channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: White House row with Ukraine raises stakes for European summit in London
item.description: LONDON (AP) — It was supposed to cap a week of whirlwind diplomacy advancing the prospect of peace in Ukraine. But a summit of European leaders on Sunday has been overshadowed by the extraordinary scolding by U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Syrians begin fasting during first Ramadan without Assad family rule in decades
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Some restaurants and coffee shops in Syria were closed during the day Saturday while others opened as usual as observant Muslims began fasting during the holy month of Ramadan, the first since the fall of Assad family rule in the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: ABDULRAHMAN SHAHEEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Connor Zilisch's 'not smart' moves lead him to victory at Focused Health 250 in Austin
item.description: Zilisch shrugged off early and late trouble to capture the checkered flag in the NASCAR Xfinity Series Focused Health 250 on Saturday at Circuit of the Americas. It's his second career win on NASCAR's second circuit.
item.dc:creator: Grace Reader, WFLA, NBC

item.title: About 8 in 10 say they've recently experienced extreme weather: Survey
item.description: Overall, nearly 70 percent of adults believe in climate change, with 93 percent of Democrats and 51 percent of Republicans saying so, poll results show.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KREX 5

item.title: Lifelong New York civil rights advocate and NAACP leader Hazel Dukes dies at 92
item.description: Hazel Dukes, the president of the New York State chapter of the NAACP and lifelong civil rights advocate, died Saturday at the age of 92. Dukes peacefully passed away in her New York City home surrounded by family, her son, Ronald Dukes, said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump gender-affirming care federal funding ban
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King ruled that President Trump’s executive orders violate the Constitution’s Fifth Amendment, which grants equal protection under the law.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Lee Kunzman, 'persistent' Indy 500 veteran, dies at 80
item.description: Kunzman's best finish in his INDYCAR SERIES career was in 1979, when he placed second at Atlanta Motor Speedway. It was just a narrow loss to winner Johnny Rutherford, IMS officials said.
item.dc:creator: Cooper Hudson, NewsNation

item.title: Threatening letter sent to northern Colorado family over MAGA banner
item.description: A Severance family says they recently received a threatening letter in the mail, in response to a "Make America Great Again" banner posted in their front yard.
item.dc:creator: Greg Nieto, KRON4

item.title: FedEx cargo plane makes emergency landing following bird strike and engine fire
item.description: A FedEx cargo plane made an emergency landing at a busy New Jersey airport on Saturday after a bird strike caused an engine fire that could be seen in the morning sky.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cuomo jumps into New York mayor's race, attempting political comeback
item.description: Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo (D) announced Saturday afternoon that he's entering New York City’s mayoral race, adding a big name to the already busy Democratic primary challenging Mayor Eric Adams (D).
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge blocks Trump order threatening funding for institutions that offer care for transgender youth
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump’s plan to pull federal funding from institutions that provide gender-affirming care for transgender youth will remain blocked on a long-term basis under a federal judge's ruling in Seattle late Friday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Barbie celebrates the world of music with 2025 Career of the Year, Alicia Keys doll
item.description: Barbie is celebrating all things music this year, announcing its 2025 Career of the Year and a new doll, highlighting a popular musical artist.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alabama governor commutes death row inmate Rocky Myers' sentence to life in prison
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey commuted the death sentence of Robin “Rocky” Myers to life in prison Friday, saying there were enough questions about his guilt that she could not move forward with his execution.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Video shows implosion of iconic Six Flags Great Adventure rollercoaster Kingda Ka
item.description: The king has fallen. The legendary Kingda Ka ride at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey was imploded on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah senators react to contentious Oval Office meeting between Trump and Zelenskyy
item.description: Utah's senators are responding to a contentious interaction between Trump and Vance and Zelenskyy Friday with contrasting public statements.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jury finds Illinois landlord guilty of murder, hate crime in 2023 attack on Palestinian American boy
item.description: JOLIET, Ill. (AP) — A jury found an Illinois landlord guilty of murder and hate crime charges Friday for the brutal killing of a 6-year-old whose mother rented rooms in the man’s home, an attack that spiked fears over anti-Muslim discrimination in the earliest days of the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and MELISSA PEREZ WINDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Zelenskyy thanks Trump for visit after Oval Office clash
item.description: The meeting escalated after Vice President JD Vance suggested a diplomatic solution could be reached with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Haitian man charged in NC triple murder flew into US under Biden migrant flights program: ICE
item.description: ICE said a Haitian migrant, who had flown into the US as part of former President Biden's migrant flight program, was arrested and charged with triple murder in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missouri House reviews bill to ban NDAs in child abuse cases
item.description: The bill being considered is aiming to provide a "thread of hope" for survivors and prevent further silence and trauma.
item.dc:creator: Parker Padgett, NewsNation

item.title: Patrolling America's 'third border' from the skies
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard is taking to the skies to monitor Florida's coast as a crackdown on illegal immigration continues.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Wyoming's only full-service clinic stops providing abortions after new regulations signed
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyol (AP) — Wyoming's only full-service abortion clinic stopped providing abortions Friday, but it remained open after Gov. Mark Gordon approved a law requiring that such facilities be licensed as surgical centers.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Leonard Peltier remains defiant in AP interview, maintaining innocence and vowing continued activism
item.description: BELCOURT, N.D. (AP) — More than 50 years after a shootout on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation landed him in federal prison, Leonard Peltier remains defiant.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pope had bronchial spasm, resulted in him inhaling vomit, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered a bronchial spasm on Friday that resulted in him breathing in vomit, requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying a setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Musicians, bus driver caught with 200 pounds of cocaine
item.description: Officers sent the bus to secondary inspection after attempting to cross the Hidalgo-Reynosa International Bridge, where the discovery was made.
item.dc:creator: Dave Hendricks, NewsNation

item.title: Where things stand as Congress tries to avoid a partial government shutdown in two weeks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As House members finished voting for the week and left Washington, the lead Democrat on the House Appropriations Committee, Rep.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Volunteers use bullhorns and sirens to warn immigrants when ICE is in their area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Just before dawn, 10 people met at a parking lot shared by a laundromat and coffee shop in South Central Los Angeles on what has become a daily mission: Look for immigration officers and warn people of their presence to try to prevent arrests.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: EPA backtracks on Trump comment about cutting staff by 65% but says major spending cuts are coming
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Despite a comment by President Donald Trump, the Environmental Protection Agency does not plan to reduce its staff by 65%, the White House and the agency said Thursday, though major budget cuts are likely.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As Trump's deadline to eliminate DEI nears, few schools openly rush to make changes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Schools and colleges across the U.S. face a Friday deadline to end diversity programs or risk having their federal money pulled by the Trump administration, yet few are openly rushing to make changes.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, JOCELYN GECKER and CHEYANNE MUMPHREY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump shares AI video envisioning Gaza development with gold statue of himself
item.description: The video also features children holding gold balloons also made to look like Trump and a renovated beachfront with luxury hotels and money flying in the air.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9
